title: SECI Cancels Green Hydrogen Hub Tender Under Indiaâ€™s National Hydrogen Mission
content: Indiaâ€™s Solar Energy Corporation (SECI) has officially pulled the plug on its previously announced call for proposals to develop green hydrogen hubs, delivering a blow to the countryâ€™s highly publicised National Green Hydrogen Mission. The termination, disclosed by SECI on July 4, cancels a tender initially floated on August 20, 2024, that sought to jumpstart large-scale hydrogen ecosystems across the nation. Under the original plan, SECI had earmarked a total budget of two billion rupees (INR 2bn, approx. $23.3 million) to support at least two hydrogen hubs, each expected to produce a minimum of 100,000 tonnes of green hydrogen annually. Based on typical electrolyser performance and upstream renewables supply, this scale of hydrogen output would have required several hundred megawatts of installed electrolyser capacity â€” a tall order for Indiaâ€™s emerging hydrogen market.Official notice can be read here: https://www.seci.co.in/uploads/news/1751611247_CfP_cancellation_notice.pdf
author: Analysts Account
